objective_statement: A journalist under a pseudonym has published major investigative work for ~15 years.
The journalist has a highly respected public reputation.
No verified photograph of the journalist exists.
The reporting has been accurate enough to attract important sources.
Whistleblowers and insiders have trusted the identity with sensitive information.
Different people describe the journalist's voice or physical presence differently.
It is not established that those describing encounters all met the same person.
The identity may function as a mantle, collective, digital persona, or myth.
The journalist announced an article that would allegedly change the course of AI and humanity.
The article was not published as scheduled.
At least part of the article may exist.
Different parties want the article published, suppressed, purchased, verified, or destroyed.
The article's exact subject is unconfirmed and contested.
The article is the central object many characters want to control.
The journalist disappeared shortly before the article's planned publication.
The journalist's death has not been confirmed.
Voluntary disappearance remains possible.
Murder or forced disappearance remains possible.
The disappearance may refer to a person, an identity, an AI system, or a network ceasing activity.
For several years the identity regularly ordered food from the same restaurant.
The delivery was always sent to the same New York apartment building.
No apartment number was included.
Delivery instructions said to leave the food in the lobby.
The delivery worker marked or messaged that the order had arrived.
The order was deliberately timed to arrive near the end of the doorman's shift.
The doorman's shift ended shortly after the delivery.
The doorman never saw who collected the food.
The overnight staff did not normally see the food remain in the lobby.
No known witness has definitively seen the recipient collect the food.
The food was gone by the following morning.
The restaurant owner or records can establish the repeated pattern.
This does not prove the journalist lived in the building.
This does not prove the same person collected each order.
This does not prove the journalist personally ate the food.
The restaurant/bodega delivery pattern is a core, must-keep fact.
The apartment building is connected to the food-delivery routine.
Enough people pass through that entering or leaving does not establish residency.
The journalist's pseudonym is not necessarily known to building management.
The lobby operates as an accidental or intentional anonymity mechanism.
Shift changes are important to the absence of a direct witness.
Building security records may be incomplete.
A period of surveillance footage may be missing, deleted, corrupted, or unavailable.
The journalist communicates remotely through secure or encrypted systems.
May have used voice, text, email, secure-drop, or AI-mediated communication.
Different communication channels may present different apparent identities.
Some communications may have been scheduled rather than sent live.
Someone may have impersonated the journalist.
An authentic account does not prove the human author was present.
The journalist's digital infrastructure may continue functioning after the disappearance.
The published work has a recognizable style.
Some experts read the consistent style as evidence of one author.
Other experts read variations as evidence of multiple authors.
AI-assisted editing, shared standards, imitation, or model prose could explain either.
Writing analysis provides evidence but not a definitive answer.
The article may materially affect AI companies, investors, markets, policy, and public trust.
Financial actors have reason to learn whether the article is authentic.
Technology companies have reason to control its release.
Journalists and public-interest figures have reason to publish it.
An unusual financial transaction / market position shortly before the disappearance has been proposed.
A Thursday-night encrypted chatroom appearance.
A missed weekly AI chat.
A large cryptocurrency transfer immediately before the disappearance.
Twenty-three minutes of deleted building footage.
Taxi trips billed to the journalist.
Rare-library-book reservations.
Dry-cleaning pickups under the pseudonym.
A long-running PO box.
Annual charity donations signed 'Questions matter'.
One disputed photograph (never verified; may be anyone).
Multiple people collecting clothing or documents.
A guest Wi-Fi connection at the apartment building.
Changing device identifiers.
An actor CLAIMS a past romance with the journalist -- disputed and uncorroborated (the claimant admits, privately, it may be fabricated).
A meeting was proposed or a newsroom summons was sent, but attendance was never confirmed (second-hand for C10/C14; no one attests the journalist actually appeared).
A specific final message naming a successor.
A hidden locker or physical dead drop.
Forensic timing analysis of the byline's responses shows near-zero-variance latency and no diurnal/timezone/sleep cycle, sustained for years.
Forensic check: the successor-naming message carries the same scheduled/automated signature as F046/F049 -- it could be machine-emitted or self-authored.

At least two 'journalist' breadcrumb trails trace to different unrelated real people (e.g., the taxi account to a real-estate agent, the library reservations to a grad student) who never knew each other or the byline.

The byline may have begun as an in-house editorial pseudonym / desk brand the paper attached to miscellaneous and freelance pieces, with a biography accreting over time (contested; C03 may misremember or be self-serving).
